title: 2007 The Mystery of the Bayon Face Towers
highlights: essay examines the cultural and religious shifts in the Bàyon temple complex during and after the reign of Jayavarma Parameśvara, focusing on the iconography of the face towers. highlights: Bayon period, particularly during the reign of King Jayavarman VII (1181-1218 CE), represents a fascinating epoch in Khmer history. This era is renowned for its syncretism, where Hindu and Buddhist elements coexisted and intermingled  ...    Vishnu, one of the principal deities in Hinduism, played a significant role during this period, reflecting the  ...    Jayavarman VII’s Reign: Jayavarman VII was a fervent follower of Mahayana Buddhism and is celebrated for his monumental architectural contributions, including the Bayon temple in Angkor Thom. Despite his Buddhist inclinations, the period saw the continued reverence for Hindu deities, illustrating the inclusive and syncretic nature of Khmer religious life.
